title: Regent Seven Seas Baltic & Scandinavia Cruises 2026
intro: Regent Seven Seas Cruises delivers the most inclusive luxury experience in the Baltic & Scandinavia, with every voyage featuring unlimited shore excursions, fine wines and spirits, gourmet dining, and even business-class intercontinental flights on select bookings. Their intimate, all-suite ships navigate narrow waterways and historic ports inaccessible to larger vessels, from Stockholm's archipelago to St. Petersburg's Neva River. The 2026 season showcases extended overnight stays in cultural capitals, allowing deeper immersion in Scandinavian design, Russian imperial heritage, and medieval Hanseatic trading posts. With gratuities, Wi-Fi, valet laundry, and pre-cruise hotel nights included, guests experience seamless exploration without wallet anxiety. Regent's hallmark is personalized service—nearly one staff member per guest—ensuring every Baltic sunrise and Scandinavian fjord excursion unfolds with understated elegance.

faqs.question: How much does a Regent Seven Seas Baltic & Scandinavia cruise cost?
faqs.answer: Regent Seven Seas Baltic & Scandinavia cruises start from €5,499 per person for 10-night shoulder-season voyages, with peak summer sailings ranging €6,999-€8,499 for entry-level suites. Penthouse suites and above typically run €12,499-€25,000 depending on itinerary length and season. The fare includes business-class intercontinental flights, unlimited shore excursions, all beverages, gratuities, and pre-cruise hotel nights—making true out-of-pocket expenses minimal. Single travelers face 25-75% supplements, though occasional no-single-supplement promotions appear 6-9 months before sailing.

faqs.question: Which Regent Seven Seas ships sail to Baltic & Scandinavia?
faqs.answer: Three ships dominate Regent's 2026 Baltic & Scandinavia deployment: Seven Seas Splendor (2020) brings the newest luxury amenities and largest suites; Seven Seas Voyager offers proven Baltic expertise with its 700-guest intimate scale; and Seven Seas Navigator (490 guests) accesses smaller ports like Visby and Bornholm that larger ships cannot reach. All feature all-suite, all-balcony accommodations and the line's signature inclusive experience. Splendor and Voyager handle peak summer capacity, while Navigator specializes in shoulder-season cultural immersion itineraries with longer port stays.

faqs.question: When is best time to cruise Baltic & Scandinavia?
faqs.answer: June through August delivers warmest weather (18-22°C), longest daylight hours extending past 10pm, and fully operational attractions, though crowds peak and prices rise 25-30%. May and September offer excellent value with blooming gardens or autumn colors, fewer tourists, and still-comfortable 12-18°C temperatures—plus occasional Northern Lights in late September. November sailings target Northern Lights seekers but risk stormy seas and limited shore excursion availability. Avoid March-April when Baltic ice can disrupt itineraries and many museums remain closed for renovation.

faqs.question: What is included?
faqs.answer: Regent's fare includes virtually everything: all-suite accommodations with private balconies, unlimited shore excursions in every port (typically 3-5 options daily), all beverages including premium spirits and wines, gourmet dining across multiple venues including specialty restaurants, gratuities, unlimited Wi-Fi, valet laundry, ground transfers, and pre-cruise hotel nights on most voyages. Intercontinental bookings include round-trip business-class airfare. Extras are limited to spa treatments, casino, boutique purchases, and occasional premium excursions. This all-inclusive model eliminates onboard spending anxiety and simplifies budgeting—rarely will guests exceed €200 additional for a week.

faqs.question: Which ports?
faqs.answer: Regent's Baltic & Scandinavia itineraries feature Copenhagen, Stockholm, Helsinki, and St. Petersburg as cornerstone ports with overnight stays allowing deep cultural immersion. Tallinn, Berlin (Warnemünde), and Riga provide medieval Hanseatic heritage. Norwegian fjord sailings add Flåm, Bergen, and Geiranger. Smaller calls include Gdańsk for Polish maritime history, Visby on Gotland for preserved medieval architecture, and Bornholm for Danish island culture. St. Petersburg requires visa-free arrangements through organized excursions—a significant Regent advantage. Amsterdam, London (Southampton), and occasional Oslo serve as alternative embarkation points for varied routing options.

faqs.question: How far ahead to book?
faqs.answer: Book Regent Baltic & Scandinavia cruises 9-18 months ahead for optimal suite selection, particularly for Penthouse categories and above which sell first. Early booking incentives typically offer 10-15% savings or onboard credits. Shoulder-season sailings in May and September can be secured 6-9 months out with reasonable availability. Last-minute deals are rare given Regent's small fleet and loyal repeat clientele, though occasional single-supplement waivers appear 3-6 months before sailing. For 2026 peak summer voyages, booking by late 2024 ensures preferred dining times and excursion availability, particularly for limited-capacity experiences like Hermitage private tours.

faqs.question: Good for families?
faqs.answer: Regent Seven Seas caters primarily to affluent adults and couples, with limited dedicated children's programming compared to mass-market lines. However, multigenerational families find success during summer school holidays when Club Mariner youth program operates for ages 5-17, featuring supervised activities and shore excursions adapted for younger travelers. The all-inclusive model appeals to families seeking predictable costs, and spacious suites accommodate third and fourth guests in select categories. Educational shore excursions—particularly in St. Petersburg and Scandinavian capitals—engage curious teenagers. Families should request connecting suites and book early, as family-suitable accommodations represent under 10% of inventory.

faqs.question: Cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Regent Seven Seas applies a tiered cancellation schedule: full refund minus €150 administrative fee for cancellations 151+ days before sailing; deposit forfeiture (typically 25% of fare) for cancellations 150-121 days out; 50% penalty for 120-91 days; 75% for 90-61 days; and 100% forfeiture within 60 days of departure. Travel insurance is strongly recommended given substantial penalties—Regent offers comprehensive coverage including cancel-for-any-reason upgrades. COVID-19 protocols remain flexible with future cruise credits offered for positive tests within 10 days of sailing. Rebooking to alternative sailings typically incurs €75 change fee if outside penalty window.
